steps.title: Calculate the balance after 4 months
steps.content:
The account starts with 700 dollars and earns 0.6% of the original balance in the first 4 months.
Interest earned: $$0.006 \times 700 = 4.20$$
Balance after 4 months: $$B(4) = 700 + 4.20 = 704.20$$

steps.title: Identify the growth pattern every 2 months
steps.content:
After month 4, the account earns 0.1% every 2 months, applied to the prior balance. This multiplies the balance by $1.001$ every 2 months:
  • At month 6: Balance = $704.20 \times 1.001$
  • At month 8: Balance = $704.20 \times (1.001)^2$
  • At month 10: Balance = $704.20 \times (1.001)^3$

steps.title: Write the formula with exponent in terms of x
steps.content:
Each 2-month period represents one factor of $1.001$. Since $x$ months have passed since month 4, the number of complete 2-month periods is $\frac{x-4}{2}$.
Therefore: $$B(x) = 704.20(1.001)^{\frac{x-4}{2}}$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
